publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-04-05 23:03:46 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Department of Chemistry records
did.physdesc: Photographs, articles, lecture notes, memorabilia, correspondence, tape recordings, programs, material on professorial exchanges, and announcements.
did.abstract: Historical material relating to the Chemistry Department includes photographs (including prints, and glass and film slides), articles, lecture notes, memorabilia, correspondence, tape recordings, programs, material on professorial exchanges, and announcements.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Historical material relating to the Chemistry Department includes photographs (including prints, and glass and film slides), articles, lecture notes, memorabilia, correspondence, tape recordings, programs, material on professorial exchanges, and announcements; also, photographs documenting the construction of Baker Laboratory; and a history of the department by Emile Chamot and Fred H. Rhodes.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Department of Chemistry Records, #14-8-2535. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Not all the material in the collection is covered by this guide.

did.unittitle: Ananth Group webcrawl
Series I. Correspondence
Brauner, Bohuslav to Dennis, Louis M.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prague; Brauner (1855-1935) was a Czech chemist; one original plus one photostat

did.unittitle: Hempel, Walther to Dennis, Louis M.
Chamot, Emile Monnin to his mother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ithaca; includes references to the fire in Morse Hall that had just occurred

did.unittitle: Sidgwick, Nevil Vincent to Debye, Peter
Sidgwick, Nevil Vincent to Debye, Peter
Sidgwick, Nevil Vincent to Debye, Peter
Sidgwick, Nevil Vincent to Johnson, John R.
Sidgwick, Nevil Vincent to Johnson, John R.
Sidgwick, Nevil Vincent to Laubengayer, Albert W.
Willstaetter, Richard to Dennis, Louis M.
Crafts, James Mason to Fessenden, J. P., MD

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Boston; Crafts was the first Professor of Chemistry at Cornell; concerns Crafts family genealogy

did.unittitle: Fessenden, Elizabeth, Cornell class of 1936 (Mrs. Newell W. Washburn) to Laubengayer, Albert W.
Debye, Peter to Hildebrand, Joel
Hildebrand, Joel to Widom, Benjamin
Dennis, Louis M. to Bostwick, Charles D.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: notarized; Dennis was Chemistry department chair, 1903-1931; Bostwick was University Treasurer; concerns lab accident to Louise Margaret Hamburger, BS Ag, 1919

did.unittitle: Bunsen, Robert W. to or about Desaga, Peter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed transcript of original May 12, 1855 letter in which Bunsen credits Peter Desaga (Mechanician at the University of Heidelberg) with the actual design of the Bunsen burner

did.unittitle: Dennis, Louis M. and Schoermer & Teichmann, dealer in Hamburg, Germany

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence re purchase of original Bunsen letter; plus exhibit cards describing the original letter, when it was on display in Baker Lab; location of original letter remains unknown as of November 2021

did.unittitle: Series II. Printed material; other documents
"Temperature of maximum density of water"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mss., possibly by Wilder D. Bancroft; research project assigned by Bancroft, and sent by his student, Frederick Adler, B. Chem., 1926

did.unittitle: Salaries of Chemistry Dept. professors, instructors, and assistants

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: from Charles D. Bostwick, University Treasurer

did.unittitle: American Chemical Society (ACS), Cornell Section charter
American Chemical Society (ACS), Cornell Section; meeting at Cornell program book
American Chemical Society (ACS), Cornell Section; correspondence and documents
"Support for a New Research Wing for the Cornell Chemistry Department"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: by Harold A. Scheraga, J. W. Hastie, and Deane W. Malott; application for NSF grad-level research facilities development grant

did.unittitle: Program statement for space needs
Dept. of Chemistry Course of Instruction
Dept. of Chemistry Announcements (two separate years)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Official Publications of Cornell University, Vol. VI, No. 11 and Vol. XII, No. 11

did.unittitle: Floor plan of Baker Laboratory of Chemistry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 pp., rolled up; in great detail

did.unittitle: "The George Fisher Baker Laboratory of Chemistry, Cornell University"
Plans of Baker Laboratory
George Fisher Baker Non-resident Lectureship in Chemistry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: one folder of correspondence and documents

did.unittitle: George Fisher Baker Non-resident Lectureship in Chemistry: General Addresses of Baker Lecturers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: General title for the following thirteen entries; some years lacking

did.unittitle: "Ancient and Modern Alchemy," Fritz Paneth
"What Can the Modern Chemist Learn from the Old Alchemy?," Paul Walden
"Problems and Methods in Enzyme Research," Richard Willstaetter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Funded from the Baker Lectureship, but he was not a Baker Lecturer

did.unittitle: "International Relations in Science," George Barger
"Twenty-five Years of Biochemistry," Hans Pringsheim
"The Present and Future State of our Natural Resources," F. M. Jaeger
"Waves and Particles," G. P. Thomson
"The Development of Views Regarding the Nature of Chemical Forces," Kasimir Fajans
"The Age of the Earth," Georg von Hevesy
"The Relation of Physics to Chemistry," Nevil Vincent Sidgwick
"Pure and Applied Science," Cecil H. Desch
"From the Ponderable to the Imponderable," Otto Hahn
"The Physical Sciences," W. L. Bragg
Minutes of the Faculty of the Department of Chemistry
Minutes of the Faculty of the Department of Chemistry
Series III. Photographs
Chemistry faculty, 1901-02

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: in album; these and other group photos in album are primarily cyanotypes

did.unittitle: Dept. of Chemistry, 1912-13
Dept. of Chemistry
Dept. of Chemistry, seniors
Dept. of Chemistry, seniors
"Nitrogen evolution"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 graphs; in album with photos above

did.unittitle: American Chemical Society meeting at Cornell: group portrait of attendees in front of Baker Lab

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: by John P. Troy; very long and rolled up

Cornell scenes; Cornell faculty
Morse Hall; faculty; Cornell

Fritz Paneth, Baker Lecturer
Hans Pringsheim, Baker Lecturer

C. K. (Sir Christopher Kelk) Ingold, Baker Lecturer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: title for this and eleven succeeding entries

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: oversize, mounted; title for this and six succeeding entries

Department of Chemistry (Faculty, Staff, and Students) group portraits
Department of Chemistry (Faculty, Staff, and Students) group portraits
Department of Chemistry (Faculty, Staff, and Students) group portraits
Department of Chemistry (Faculty, Staff, and Students) group portraits

American Chemical Society, Cornell section, luncheon, Gov. Clinton Hotel
Senior Chemists (graduating seniors)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: with letter dated September 19, 1972 from Harry H. Davidson, '19, East Hampton, NY to Albert W. Laubengayer

Senior Chemists

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: with letter dated October 14, 1969 from Anthony M. Schwartz, '28, Rockville, MD to William D. Gurowitz

did.unittitle: Vincent du Vigneaud and two associates
Peter Debye
A. Strecker, G. Nichols, and unidentified (Laubengayer group members)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Secretary of Cornell section, American Chemical Society; photo by Arnold H. Fainberg

Bert Lemon (Carl Robert Lemon, ME 1931)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: with note dated June 20, 1977 from Catherine ("Bobby") Gallagher Witherell, '30, Bristol, VT to Albert W. Laubengayer

did.unittitle: "Doc" Mason (Prof. Clyde W. Mason) on Beebe Lake
Chemistry Department crew (athletics champions)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Les" Baum, Newt Jones, Herb Kathe, ? Peckham, M. F. "Spike" Witherell, Charlton Bascom, Rog Sutton; Cox: M. E. Gardner (sub.), Ted Rochow (reg.)

did.unittitle: Emile Monnin Chamot
Frederick Mason Brewer (1903-1963)
Dept. of Sanitary Chemistry water lab
Department of Chemistry, Fall 1916
Chemistry students at formal banquet
John Gamble Kirkwood
Emile Monnin Chamot
Peter Debye
Group portrait of Chemistry department faculty, staff, and students

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: J. E. Trevor; Edward L. Nichols (Physics), H. R. Carveth (Ph.D.'98), Charles G. L. Wolf; Grads '99-'00: Morris R. Ebersole, Ira H. Derby, A. T. Lincoln, and Arthur M. Saunders; Benton Dales (Ph.D. 1901); James H. Pettit ('96-'01, Ph.B '00)

did.unittitle: Group portrait of Chemistry department faculty and staff

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 cc., one with key; Professors George C. Caldwell, Louis M. Dennis, Willam R. Orndorff, Joseph E. Trevor; Assistant Professor Wilder D. Bancroft; Instructors Hector R. Carveth, Emile M. Chamot (Microchemical and Sanitary Chemistry, Toxicology), Blinn S. Cushman, Henry R. Jessel, Theodore Whittelsey; Assistants George H. Burrows, Robert Fischer, Arthur R. Middleton, George A. Smith, John E. Teeple, and Benjamin B. Turner; Stockroom keeper, Henry Feehan; plus guest: Prof. J. (Jacobus) H. Van't Hoff, first person to receive Nobel Prize in Chemistry (1901)

did.unittitle: Group portrait of Chemistry department faculty, staff, and students

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professors Bancroft, Caldwell, Chamot, Dennis, Orndorff, and Trevor; Instructors Carveth, Jessel, and Whittelsey; Grad students Bizzell, Burrows, Cushman, Dales, Fowler, Gottschalk, Harris, Middleton, Teeple, and Todd; Seniors Geer, Jennings, Lauder, and Sheperd; Junior Hawley; Freshman Wheeler

did.unittitle: Group portrait of Chemistry department faculty, staff, and students

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 prints (nearly identical poses from one sitting); James E. Trevor, F. G. Wheeler, E. W. Todd, U. G. Gottschalk, George H. Burrows, '03, C. D. Harris, '03, A. G. Lauder, '03, Henry R. Jessel, '89, F. H. Jennings, '06, Hector R. Carveth, '99, Arthur R. Middleton, '03, '04, Blinn S. Cushman, '98, '01, W. C. Geer, '98, '05, Theodore Whittelsey, E. S. Sheperd, '02, '04, J. A. Bizzell, William R. Orndorff, Louis M. Dennis, George C. Caldwell, Joseph E. Trevor, Wilder D. Bancroft

did.unittitle: Chemistry Class of 1918, in front of Morse Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: plus glass plate negative of same group, but different pose

did.unittitle: Group portrait of Chemistry department faculty, staff, and students in front of Baker Lab
Charlie Morse (Charles Walter Morse, B. Chem., '21)
Class of 1918 in front of Morse Hall
Unidentified professor, with colleague or student
Hans H. Muxfeldt, at blackboard with molecular structure of antibiotic Terramycin, which he was the first to chemically synthesize
Robert A. Plane in laboratory; playing trumpet
Franklin A. Long with George W. Rathjens (Physics, MIT)
Franklin A. Long with Raymond Bowers, (Physics, Cornell)
John R. Wiesenfeld in laboratory with student or colleague
Laboratories (unidentified)
Small portraits of students and/or faculty, identified

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carr; George F. Condike, Pittsburgh, 1943; Elliott; Harvey R. Engle, Boston, 1932; Gordon Finlay; Gilly Gilliam; Kirkwood (?); Jim Magoffin (2); Art Newkirk; James A. Robertson, Spanish Fork, Utah; Arthur Saum, Hiram College, Cleveland; Frank Schirmer; Scotty Sears; Sears and "Nick"; Charles Smith, Portland, OR, Sept. 1944; Taube; Bob Weisz; Weisz and James L. Hoard; John Whitney, August 1944

did.unittitle: Small portraits of students and/or faculty, unidentified

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: plus some photos of Chemistry department picnic, n.d., etc.

did.unittitle: Boron crystals; Chemistry department picnic
Unidentified man at laboratory bench; unidentified man at desk; headshots of ten unidentified men (students and/or faculty)
Colyer standing in front of Morse Hall
Chemistry lecture room in Franklin Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: with note: taken at 1:05 p.m. after a Newbury lecture on organic chemistry

Advanced inorganic chemistry lab, Morse Hall, Room 68
Assaying lab, Morse Hall
Electrochemistry lab, Morse Hall
Elementary inorganic chemistry lab , Morse Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: two o/s prints (one with, one without people)

Gas analysis lab, Baker
Introductory inorganic chemistry and assaying lab; (lab for organic chemistry through the doorway) in Franklin Hall Annex
Introductory inorganic chemistry lab, Baker
Introductory inorganic chemistry lab, Baker, with people
Microchemical analysis lab, Morse Hall
Organic chemistry lab, Morse Hall
Organic chemistry lab (different room from above), Franklin or Morse Hall
Physical chemistry lab, Morse Hall
Qualitative analysis lab, Franklin Hall
Quantitative analysis lab, Franklin Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: three views, including one with female student (original sepia print plus surrogate print); four prints total

did.unittitle: Quantitative analysis lab, Morse Hall
Quantitative analysis lab, Morse Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: with people; four different views in five prints (one cyanotype, two sepia, and 2 cc. from a glass negative)

did.unittitle: Sanitary chemistry lab , Morse Hall (or Laboratory of agricultural analysis)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: two identical images but with two different captions; correct one not yet determined

did.unittitle: Spectroscopic chemical analysis lab, Morse Hall
unidentified lab, Morse Hall
Experimental hood designed by Prof. Louis M. Dennis and used to test for those finally installed in Baker Lab
Jacob Papish in Dennis's lab, Morse Hall, recovering Ge Cl4 (germanium chloride) from smelter residues
Chemistry Library in Baker
Louis M. Dennis with students and colleagues in one of the graduate laboratories, Baker

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: by John Troy; 2 images (2 cc. of one)

did.unittitle: Emile M. Chamot's laboratory for chemical microscopy, Baker

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 very similar images; one from glass plate neg.

did.unittitle: Students in laboratory, possibly analyzing milk, Baker

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: mostly men; two women at far right

did.unittitle: Man with unidentified apparatus in laboratory, Baker
Five men and two women at tea/coffee break in laboratory, Baker
Large laboratory full of male students; three women in foreground
Eight students (seven men, one woman) in laboratory, Baker
Four men in unidentified lab in Baker
Albert W. Laubengayer's lab (formerly Dennis's ), with mercury vapor pump and brass meter stick, Baker
Vacuum line in unidentified lab, Baker

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: caption reads: "the first vacuum line in the U.S."; (= Schlenk line)

did.unittitle: Yvonne Meinwald in laboratory, Baker

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 photos by Charles Harrington (Visual Services job 870103, R1-Frames 12, 15, 24)

did.unittitle: Franklin A. Long
Grad student and sophomore in lab
Morse Hall after the February 1916 fire, roofed over

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: glass plate negative, donated by Harry E. Mattin

did.unittitle: unidentified labs: one without people, two with
group sitting on Morse Hall steps

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: glass plate negative; may be same as one of the prints

did.unittitle: Mearl Ossening
Glass slides: prominent historical figures in chemistry
Glass slides, various scenes, including laying cornerstone of Baker Lab, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: wooden box; prints from some of these are in Boxes 1c and 4

did.unittitle: Series IV. Artifacts and Audiovisual
Pen, with holder, used to sign plans of the new chemistry building (Baker Laboratory)
Original keys to Baker Laboratory

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: handed to Pres. Livingston Farrand by George Fisher Baker at the dedication exercises

did.unittitle: Metal plate: Periodic Table through Element 98 (Californium)
Louis M. Dennis's menu card with signatures
"Non-resident lectures" (Baker Lectures)
Interview with Peter Debye
Peter Debye's remarks at symposium banquet
